states.name: Alabama
states.notes: Alabama has one of the lowest property tax rates in the US. Homestead exemption reduces assessed value by $4,000.

states.name: Alaska
states.notes: No state property tax — taxes are set by boroughs and municipalities only. Senior exemption up to $150,000 off assessed value.

states.name: Arizona
states.notes: Arizona assesses residential property at 10% of full cash value. Two payment installments per year.

states.name: Arkansas
states.notes: Homestead credit of $375 applied directly to tax bill. Assessment ratio is 20% of market value.

states.name: California
states.notes: Proposition 13 caps annual increases at 2%. Homestead exemption up to $300,000 for qualifying seniors.

states.name: Colorado
states.notes: Residential assessment rate is 6.765%. Senior property tax exemption exempts 50% of the first $200,000.

states.name: Connecticut
states.notes: One of the highest rates in the US. No statewide homestead exemption but municipalities offer local credits.

states.name: Delaware
states.notes: Senior school property tax credit available. Very low effective rate compared to neighboring states.

states.name: Florida
states.notes: Homestead exemption up to $50,000. Save Our Homes cap limits annual assessment increases to 3% or CPI.

states.name: Georgia
states.notes: Basic homestead exemption of $2,000 off assessed value. School tax homestead exemptions also available.

states.name: Hawaii
states.notes: Lowest property tax rate in the US. Owner-occupant exemption of $100,000 off assessed value in Honolulu.

states.name: Idaho
states.notes: Homestead exemption reduces taxable value by 50% or $125,000, whichever is less.

states.name: Illinois
states.notes: Second highest rate in the US. General homestead exemption reduces EAV by $10,000 in Cook County.

states.name: Indiana
states.notes: Homestead deduction reduces assessed value by $45,000 or 60%, whichever is less.

states.name: Iowa
states.notes: Homestead credit reduces taxable value. Elderly credit available for qualifying seniors.

states.name: Kansas
states.notes: Homestead refund program for qualifying low-income seniors and disabled persons.

states.name: Kentucky
states.notes: Homestead exemption for seniors and disabled: $46,350 off assessed value in 2026.

states.name: Louisiana
states.notes: Homestead exemption of $75,000 off assessed value. Assessment at 10% of fair market value.

states.name: Maine
states.notes: Homestead exemption reduces assessed value by $25,000 for primary residence.

states.name: Maryland
states.notes: Homestead tax credit caps annual assessment increases at 10% for owner-occupied properties.

states.name: Massachusetts
states.notes: Residential exemption available in many municipalities. Seniors may qualify for additional exemptions.

states.name: Michigan
states.notes: Principal residence exemption removes school operating millage from taxable value — saves significantly.

states.name: Minnesota
states.notes: Homestead market value exclusion reduces taxable market value. First $76,000 gets 40% exclusion.

states.name: Mississippi
states.notes: Homestead exemption credit of $300 on tax bill. Additional exemption for seniors over 65.

states.name: Missouri
states.notes: No homestead exemption but senior citizens may receive property tax credit (Circuit Breaker).

states.name: Montana
states.notes: Residential property taxed at 1.35% of market value. Elderly homeowner/renter credit available.

states.name: Nebraska
states.notes: Homestead exemption for qualifying veterans, seniors, and disabled — can exempt 100% of value.

states.name: Nevada
states.notes: Assessed at 35% of taxable value. Tax abatement caps annual increases at 3% for primary residences.

states.name: New Hampshire
states.notes: No income or sales tax — property tax is primary revenue source. High rates reflect this.

states.name: New Jersey
states.notes: Highest property tax rate in the US. ANCHOR program provides up to $1,750 relief for eligible homeowners.

states.name: New Mexico
states.notes: Head of family exemption of $2,000 off assessed value. Low effective rate relative to home values.

states.name: New York
states.notes: STAR program provides school tax relief. Enhanced STAR for seniors saves up to $3,100 annually.

states.name: North Carolina
states.notes: Elderly/disabled exclusion exempts $25,000 or 50% of value, whichever is greater.

states.name: North Dakota
states.notes: Homestead credit for seniors and disabled: reduces assessed value by up to $9,000.

states.name: Ohio
states.notes: Homestead exemption reduces taxable value by $26,200 for seniors and disabled homeowners.

states.name: Oklahoma
states.notes: Homestead exemption reduces assessed value by $1,000. Assessment at 11% of fair cash value.

states.name: Oregon
states.notes: Measure 5 caps total property taxes at 1.5% of real market value. No homestead exemption.

states.name: Pennsylvania
states.notes: Homestead exclusion reduces assessed value by $18,000. Senior Tax Freeze available.

states.name: Rhode Island
states.notes: Homestead exemption varies by city. Providence offers up to $20,000 reduction in assessed value.

states.name: South Carolina
states.notes: Owner-occupied residential assessed at 4% vs 6% for non-primary. Seniors get $50,000 exemption.

states.name: South Dakota
states.notes: No income tax. Freeze on assessments for low-income elderly and disabled homeowners.

states.name: Tennessee
states.notes: Elderly low-income homeowners may have taxes frozen at current level. Assessment at 25% of value.

states.name: Texas
states.notes: No income tax. $100,000 homestead exemption on school district taxes since 2023. Over-65 exemption + freeze.

states.name: Utah
states.notes: Primary residential property taxed at 55% of fair market value. Circuit breaker for low-income seniors.

states.name: Vermont
states.notes: Income-based property tax adjustment available. Prebate/rebate system for qualifying homeowners.

states.name: Virginia
states.notes: No statewide homestead exemption. Localities may offer senior/disabled relief programs.

states.name: Washington
states.notes: Senior/disabled exemption reduces taxable value. No income tax makes property tax critical revenue.

states.name: West Virginia
states.notes: Homestead exemption of $20,000 off assessed value for qualifying residents age 65+.

states.name: Wisconsin
states.notes: Homestead credit for low-income residents. School levy credit reduces taxes on owner-occupied homes.

states.name: Wyoming
states.notes: No income tax. Residential property assessed at 9.5% of fair market value. Low overall tax burden.

states.name: District of Columbia
states.notes: Homestead deduction of $84,050 off assessed value. Senior citizen tax relief available.
